page: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server type includes 3780 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic accelerations such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in single-core CPU stress tests and multi-core Geekbench evaluations, both ranking in the top 10%. Image processing and prompt processing for small 135M language models also achieve top-tier performance. Multi-core stress tests, software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations fall into the strong top 25% category. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and prompt processing for medium 7B and large 70B language models drop to average levels, representing a performance tradeoff under heavy memory-bound or large-model workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64ads_v6 offers a high-density resource profile with balanced compute and memory allocations, complemented by substantial local storage. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The server delivers top-tier performance in single-core CPU tasks, multi-core Geekbench tests, and small-model LLM prompt processing. It also shows strong results in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. However, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and large-model LLM processing represent moderate performance tradeoffs. This server is well-suited for database hosting, enterprise applications, software development pipelines, and general-purpose workloads requiring a balanced compute-to-memory ratio and high-capacity local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and top-tier single-core processing performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM.
Includes 3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier performance in single-core CPU benchmarks and small-model LLM prompt processing.
Strong multi-core performance for database operations and software compilation.
Optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications and database hosting.
